Memory allocation functions that check results and call fatal on error, ensuring no failure returns.

# Purpose
This C header file, `xmalloc.h`, defines a set of memory allocation and string manipulation functions that enhance standard library functions by incorporating error checking. The functions `xmalloc`, `xcalloc`, `xrealloc`, `xreallocarray`, and `xrecallocarray` are designed to allocate memory and will call a fatal error handler if they fail, ensuring that they never return a null pointer. Additionally, the file provides string duplication functions `xstrdup` and `xstrndup`, as well as formatted output functions `xasprintf`, `xvasprintf`, `xsnprintf`, and `xvsnprintf`, which include format and non-null attribute checks to ensure safe and correct usage. The header also includes a macro definition for `__bounded__` to support bounded attribute annotations, enhancing the safety and reliability of the functions.
